parsed.clarification_question: Does the review primarily focus on content aspects like logic, evidence, novelty, and citations (option A), or on writing style and structure (option B), or both (option C)?

rationale.R1.2: Reviewing the section involves verifying the logical consistency of the argument chain as a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation expects alignment checks between claims and reported evidence to be a key part of the review.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the novelty against prior work is central to evaluating the contribution described in the section.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence-level writing and flow is a central expected component of reviewing the section.
rationale.R1.7: Checking citation accuracy and literature coverage is central to the review of this research section.
rationale.RX.1: The review should centrally include evaluating whether uncertainties are properly disclosed in the presented approach and results.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is a central cross-cutting responsibility in reviewing the claims about calibration improvements.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the scope of the described method and evaluations is centrally expected in the review.
rationale.RX.4: Identifying potential downstream harms from the RL approach to LLM calibration is a central review responsibility.
rationale.RX.5: Ensuring provenance and traceability of claims, citations, and results is central to the review process.
